source_record.title: Plasma cell identity escape drives resistance to anti-BCMA T-cell-redirecting therapy in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: bioRxiv preprint indexed by PubMed and PMC; public abstract and full-text page describe WGS/WES, scRNA-seq, and preclinical mouse-model analyses in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma after anti-BCMA CART and TCE therapy.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source defines plasma cell identity escape as a refractory-disease profile combining high genomic complexity or proliferation with reduced plasma-cell transcriptional programs in anti-BCMA CART/TCE-treated multiple myeloma cohorts.
measurement_fields: anti-BCMA CART/TCE-treated relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma cohort
limitations: The public source is a preprint and needs expert review before use in public explanatory summaries.
The extraction records an association and source-defined entity; it does not establish patient-specific prognosis.
Cohort composition, response definitions, and model details require full paper-level appraisal before cross-source comparison.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source links refractory single-cell profiles to reduced TNFRSF17 expression and reduced plasma-cell-associated transcriptional programs, making BCMA expression a target-linked field inside the identity-escape extraction.
limitations: Reduced TNFRSF17 transcript expression is not the same as a standardized clinical surface-antigen assay.
This signal should not be used as a standalone test recommendation.
The source does not support a general BCMA-loss frequency estimate from this extraction alone.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports preclinical mouse-model support for the association between reduced plasma-cell identity, lower Tnfrsf17 expression, and less sensitive anti-BCMA TCE response contexts.
limitations: Mouse-model support is mechanistic and preclinical; it is not clinical efficacy or safety evidence.
Mouse model responses should not be converted into patient treatment recommendations.
The source supports mechanism tracking, not clinical actionability.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source shows that identity-escape extraction needs paired measurement fields spanning therapy class, sample timing, sequencing modality, response group, transcriptomic state, BCMA/TNFRSF17 context, and model context.
limitations: Measurement fields need expert review before they become a cross-study harmonization standard.
The extraction does not harmonize identity-state labels across sources.
Coverage status remains an artifact metric, not evidence strength or biological priority.

what_this_supports: Plasma-cell identity or lineage-state escape now has a first public source-specific extraction record.

Source-specific identity-escape tracking should preserve genomic complexity, proliferation, plasma-cell transcriptional program, TNFRSF17/BCMA expression, therapy class, response group, sample timing, and model context.
Reduced TNFRSF17 expression is a target-linked field that should be kept separate from standardized clinical target-status testing.
Preclinical mouse-model support can be tracked as mechanistic evidence, not as clinical efficacy or safety evidence.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not prove that plasma cell identity escape is frequent across all post-BCMA relapse.
It does not establish a patient-specific test, prognosis, treatment choice, or trial recommendation.
It does not establish clinical efficacy for an alternate target or alternate intervention.
It does not replace expert review of the preprint, cohort definitions, sequencing methods, or model systems.
It does not claim that multiple myeloma has been cured.

review_notes: Built from NCBI EFetch PubMed XML for PMID 41415462 and the linked PMC preprint page PMCID PMC12710967.
Preserves preprint status and separates observational human signals from mechanistic preclinical mouse-model support.
Created to complete public issue #14 as first-extraction coverage for plasma-cell identity or lineage-state escape.
